title: neuro_SI_pipeline run manifest
description: Schema for run_manifest.json, the single source of truth for pipeline run status. Produced and atomically (lock-guarded) mutated by scripts/lib/manifest.py, so it is safe to poll mid-run. Two halves: 'meta' is the STATIC catalog (what phases/steps CAN run, with descriptions); 'run' is the LIVE per-run state (what DID run, with timestamps/exit codes/quality outcomes). Key distinction: a step's `status` answers 'did it RUN' (pending|running|completed|failed|skipped); its `outcome` answers 'did it PRODUCE meaningful output' (pass|warn|fail|skip|unknown), written post-hoc by step_quality.py and independent of status. All timestamps are RFC3339 / ISO-8601 with a timezone offset, second precision (e.g. 2026-06-17T14:15:23+00:00).

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Currently 1.0. Note: a few fields (runpod_pod_id, step.outcome, step.outcome_reason) were added within 1.0 without a version bump, so consumers should treat them as optional/nullable for forward+backward compatibility.

status.description: Execution state. Run-level is in practice running|completed|failed; phase/step also use pending|skipped.

outcome.description: Output-quality verdict, ORTHOGONAL to status. null until a step_quality.py probe runs. pass=meaningful output; warn=produced but suspicious; fail=empty/broken output despite running; skip=not applicable; unknown=can't tell.

timestamp.description: RFC3339 / ISO-8601 with tz offset, second precision; null when not yet set.

errorInfo.description: Captured for a failed phase/step. log_tail is best-effort (may be []).

log_tail.description: Last N non-empty lines of the step/phase log at failure time.

meta.description: Static catalog — what CAN run. Derived from the phases/*.sh scripts at init; identical across runs of the same code.

status_enum.description: The legal status values, echoed for self-describing consumers.

run.description: Live per-run state — what DID run.

step_filter.description: Which steps were requested (e.g. 'all' or a comma list).

resumed_at.description: Optional. Present only on resumed runs. ETA anchors here instead of started_at so a restart's ETA isn't computed off the original start.

current_phase.description: Phase currently executing, or null between phases / at terminal state.

pgid.description: pipeline.sh process-group ID (kills the whole tree incl. vLLM workers).

runpod_pod_id.description: Optional (added within schema 1.0). RunPod pod id when running on RunPod, else null/absent.

progress.description: Weighted completion fraction 0..1 (per-phase weights; rl/curriculum carry most). Rough — a long single step won't refresh it until it ends.

estimated_completion_at.description: ABSOLUTE projected finish time (read remaining = est - now). Snapshot at the last transition; null when not computable (progress 0 or terminal).

selected_phases.description: Phases included in THIS invocation (subset of meta.phases).

failure.description: Optional. Present only when the run failed; a convenience summary of the first failed phase/step so consumers don't walk the tree. Cleared on a later successful run.

log_file.description: Repo-relative path to the phase log; resolve against $REPO_ROOT.

error.description: Optional. Present only when status == failed.

log_file.description: Repo-relative path to the per-step log; resolve against $REPO_ROOT.

cw_log_stream.description: CloudWatch log-stream name when CW shipping is configured, else null.

outcome.description: Optional (added within schema 1.0). Quality verdict; null until probed, may be absent in older manifests.

outcome_reason.description: Optional. Human-readable reason behind `outcome` (e.g. '128 triples / 12 docs = 10.7/doc'). May be absent in older manifests.

error.description: Optional. Present only when status == failed.

failure.description: Top-level summary of the first failed phase, with the first failed step inside it (if any).

at.description: When the failure happened (step finish time if available, else phase finish time, else now).

step.description: Optional. First failed step within the phase.

step_error.description: Optional. Error for the failed step (present iff `step` is).
